faq.question: Is shipping insurance worth it?
faq.answer: If your loss/damage rate is above 1-2%, insurance pays for itself. High-value items ($200+) and fragile goods should always be insured. For low-value items under $50, self-insuring (absorbing occasional losses) is usually cheaper than per-package insurance.

faq.question: What does carrier declared value cover?
faq.answer: UPS and FedEx include $100 of declared value coverage automatically. USPS Priority includes $100 for domestic. Coverage pays actual value or declared value, whichever is less. Excluded items include jewelry, electronics, and artwork without additional coverage.

faq.question: Is the Shipping Insurance Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: Can I use this calculator on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, the Shipping Insurance Calculator is fully responsive and works on any device — phone, tablet, or desktop. No app download needed.

sections.title: What Is the Shipping Insurance Calculator?
sections.body: The Shipping Insurance Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your declared value, insurance provider, number of packages, you get instant results including insurance/package, total insurance cost, cost as % of value. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting insurance/package right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your declared value and need to find the right insurance/package. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
